cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Sally has 3 brothers. So, that means she has three male siblings. Now, each of those brothers has 2 sisters. Hmm, wait, if each brother has two sisters, does that mean there are two girls in the family besides Sally? Or is Sally one of those sisters? So, the question is asking how many sisters Sally has. If each brother has two sisters, then Sally's brothers have Sally and one other sister as their sisters. That would mean Sally has one sister. But wait, could there be more? Wait, the problem says Sally has 3 brothers. So the siblings are Sally, her 3 brothers, and any other sisters. Each brother has 2 sisters. So if each brother has two sisters, those two sisters must be Sally and one other sister. Therefore, Sally has one sister. So the answer is 1. Let me check
